task_completion.description: Verify that 'Global Mean Temperature' is created as a central node in the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io canvas for a shape containing the exact text 'Global Mean Temperature'.

task_completion.description: Verify that all three feedback loops (Ice-Albedo, Permafrost Carbon, Water Vapor) are drawn and connect back to the central temperature node.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three distinct cycles of nodes connected by arrows. You should see nodes related to Arctic Sea Ice, Permafrost Thaw, and Ocean Evaporation forming loops with the central 'Global Mean Temperature' node.

task_completion.description: Verify that the connectors (arrows) between nodes are curved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the lines connecting the shapes. They must be smooth, curved lines rather than straight, sharp-angled, or orthogonal (stair-step) lines.

task_completion.description: Verify that every arrow has a '+' or '-' polarity label.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look closely at every connecting arrow in the diagram. There should be a text label containing either a '+' or a '-' on or immediately adjacent to each arrow.

task_completion.description: Verify that an 'R' label is placed inside each of the three loops.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the empty space enclosed by each of the three circular node paths. There must be a text label 'R' (indicating Reinforcing) inside each of the three loops.

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagram was saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .png file to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Desktop background or a file manager window for two files: 'climate_loops.drawio' and 'climate_loops.png'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open with its toolbars, shape panels, and menus visible, rather than just a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use a script (e.g., Python with Graphviz/NetworkX) to generate the diagram and bypass manual creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal windows for python scripts, dot commands, or other programmatic graph generation tools. The diagram should be built manually in draw.io.

integrity.description: Ensure the PNG was exported from draw.io.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'Export as' dialog or evidence that the PNG matches the exact visual styling of the draw.io canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must contain exactly 3 reinforcing loops centered on 'Global Mean Temperature'. Loop 1 (Ice-Albedo): Temperature -> Arctic Sea Ice Extent -> Planetary Albedo -> Solar Energy Absorption -> Temperature. Loop 2 (Permafrost): Temperature -> Permafrost Thaw Rate -> Atmospheric CO2 & Methane -> Greenhouse Effect -> Temperature. Loop 3 (Water Vapor): Temperature -> Ocean Evaporation -> Atmospheric Water Vapor -> Greenhouse Effect -> Temperature. All arrows must be curved, have a '+' or '-' label, and each of the 3 loops must have an 'R' in the center. Expected output files are 'climate_loops.drawio' and 'climate_loops.png' on the Desktop.
